programmes.show_description: Movie Tonight: Colonel Potter hopes a movie will cure the 4077th's morale problem. But during the show, the staff of the camp are forced to make its own entertainment.
Souvenirs: Hawkeye chastises Willie Stratton, a souvenir junk dealer, for endangering the lives of Korean children. Klinger becomes a pole-sitter in his latest attempt to get discharged.
Post Op: Blood is low and casualties are high as incoming patients arrive at the 4077th. Hawkeye, BJ, Frank, and Hot Lips work in the OR through the night and into the morning with no rest.
High Spirits: Cynical about Matthew and Fiona's belief in ghosts, Henry accepts the challenge to stay in a haunted, abandoned railway station overnight.
Rumpole and the Female of the Species: Rumpole defends Tony Timson, who is accused of being involved in a bungled armed robbery. Meanwhile, Rumpole tries to get Fiona a job at his chambers.
Thoroughbreds: Part 2: Lizzy undergoes tests and the results are both shocking and unexpected. She decides not to tell Garth until she can find out what the odds are of her survival.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Song for Jason: Part 1: Jonathan and Mark become counsellors at a summer camp for children with cancer. They encounter an angry teenage athlete and a boy with an overprotective mother.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Hotel Of Dreams: Mark and Jonathan work at a hotel to help a millionaire's son dubbed Rudd the Dud find meaning in his life. They must also contend with a tyrannical boss and snooty guests.
Another Song For Christmas: In this variation on 'A Christmas Carol', Jonathan and Mark have a Dickens of a time bringing Holiday joy into the heart of 'Honest Eddie', a crooked car dealer.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Little Girl Lost: Carrie Ingalls's is in trouble when she falls into a deep hole whilst chasing a butterfly. Will a stranger come to her rescue?

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Journey in the Spring: Part 1: On hearing that his mother has passed away, Charles Ingalls's tries to convince his father to move to Walnut Grove and live with his family. Part 1 of 2.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Journey in the Spring: Part 2: Life in Walnut Grove with Charles, Caroline and his grandchildren seems to be just what Grandpa Ingalls needed after the loss of his wife. Part 2 of 2.
The Children's Carol: Part 1: Olivia's faith is tested by the horrors of the oncoming war and by recent events within the community. Part 1 of 2.
The Children's Carol: Part 2: Olivia's faith is tested by the horrors of the oncoming war and by recent events within the community. Part 2 of 2.
Brendan's Crossing: When the headmaster's job becomes available, Brendan feels it's time to improve himself but faces some stiff competition.
Never Seek to Tell: The Cosgroves prepare for their latest foster child. Nick shelters Albert Tooley, an army deserter whom Mary Pilling fears he has run away to be with.

The unwavering faith of the all-American family faces its toughest test yet when mother Olivia is struck down by polio. The clan anxiously wait to see if she will regain the use of her legs by Easter.
Margaret's Marriage: After Frank criticises Margaret for her long engagement, she calls her fiancé, Lt Col Donald Penobscott, and they set a date for the wedding at the 4077th.
Fade Out, Fade In - Part 1: Margaret's wedding leaves Major Frank Burns feeling distraught and he is sent for some R&R in Tokyo. While there he drunkenly accosts a blonde officer.
Fade Out, Fade In - Part 2: Major Charles Emerson is sent to the 4077th as a replacement by a superior officer. Charles is a feisty, highbrow surgeon and hates the conditions at the camp.
Conspiracy thriller with Denzel Washington and Meg Ryan. A US Army officer, despondent about a deadly mistake he made, investigates a female chopper pilot's worthiness for the Medal of Honor. (1996)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Fear Merchants: The Avengers' latest case sees them enter the world of ceramics when industry executives are mysteriously killed. Can Steed and Peel unearth the truth?

The Real Thing: Henry offers words of advice to Matthew when his girlfriend breaks-up with him because she has feelings for an unknown, older gentleman.
Sleeping Partners: Robin Tripp, now a fully qualified, unemployed chef, harbours ambitions of opening his own bistro...for people who appreciate high prices.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Song for Jason: Part 2: Jonathan encourages Jason's dad, Miki, to get to know his son a little bit better, but Miki would much rather forget he has a son altogether.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Bless the Boy in Blue: Mark asks God to make Jonathan a police officer for a while. After 40 years, Jonathan becomes human only to realise the harsh realities of being a cop.

The Pride of Walnut Grove: Mary worries that she will disappoint Walnut Grove if she doesn't place first in the state maths competition after the community pays her way to represent them.
A Matter of Faith: Caroline sends her family on ahead to the picnic grounds for the weekend while she nurses a nasty cut to her leg that becomes infected.
The Runaway Caboose: At a train station, Mary, Laura and Carl wander into a caboose. Without warning, the caboose begins to move, and it is soon on a crash course with an oncoming train.
Troublemaker: When Miss Beadle is deemed unable to control some of the older boys in her class, Mrs Oleson convinces the school board to replace her with a firmer, male hand.
The Long Road Home: Charles and Mr Edwards take a dangerous job handling highly explosive liquid dynamite.
For My Lady: Caroline and the girls think the worst when Charles begins to behave mysteriously and lies about the amount of time he has spent doing carpentry with an attractive neighbour.
The Recluse: Jason befriends a mysterious recluse who loves music, and Ben leaves home to look for a new job.
The Warrior: The startling history of Walton's Mountain leads to the discovery of an ancient burial ground.
The Seashore: The Waltons go on vacation to the Baldwins' beach cottage, where they meet a mysterious English girl. Ben tries to enjoy the carefree life when he is left home alone.
The Volunteer: John and Olivia must accept that Erin is growing up when G.W. Haines proposes marriage.
The First Casualty: The war deeply affects the Waltons when Curt is called into active duty and G.W. Haines is killed during training.

Rumpole and the Genuine Article: Rumpole defends an unknown artist who is accused of forging a famous painting.
